Spatial analysis of human and murine Fanconi anemia tumors

Pablo Siliceo Portugal
National Autonomous University of Mexico (UNAM)

Patients with FA have an increased predisposition to head and neck squamous cell carcinomas (HNSCC)

The tumor microenvironment alostasis is highly influenced by a crosstalk with immune surveilance

Zhang, J., Veeramachaneni, N. Targeting interleukin-1β and inflammation in lung cancer. Biomark Res 10, 5 (2022). https://doi.org/10.1186/s40364-021-00341-5

Zheng, S., Wang, W., Shen, L. et al. Tumor battlefield within inflamed, excluded or desert immune phenotypes: the mechanisms and strategies. Exp Hematol Oncol 13, 80 (2024). https://doi.org/10.1186/s40164-024-00543-1

General aim

Identify the underlying functional dynamics driving immune evasion in FA tumors by means of spatial proteomics

Tumors are complex cellular ecosystems that are in constant interaction with our immune system

Take home conclusions

FA tumors show compartmentalized expression of exhaustion and functional cell markers compared to non FA tumors

M2 macrophages infiltrate preferentially the epithelium of the FA tumor, while dendritic cells the adjacent stroma, driving a two tier exhaustion process

TDA showed that FA tumors have a distinct topological organization compared to non-FA tumors
